news.title: Maersk (CPSE:MAERSK B) Resumes Suez Shipping With Hapag Lloyd On Key Asia Route
news.summary: Maersk, traded as CPSE:MAERSK B, has resumed Asia to Mediterranean services through the Red Sea and Suez Canal. The company is operating the route in partnership with Hapag-Lloyd after earlier security concerns had redirected vessels. news.title: Shippers Maersk and Hapag-Lloyd begin return to Suez Canal trade route
news.summary:
STORY: Shipping giants Maersk and Hapag-Lloyd will resume some sailing through the Suez Canal.

That's according to a Maersk statement on Monday.

The Danish firm said it will come under their joint network called Gemini.

The Asia-Europe trade corridor through the Suez Canal was abandoned by most shippers in recent years after attacks in the Red Sea by Yemen's Houthis.

news.title: 📈 Earnings Snippet: Maersk
news.summary: Despite the disruptions caused by the Mideast war, Maersk today upgraded its guidance, citing strong demand, particularly in East Asia, and higher freight rates. The shipping giant expects volumes in the container market to grow by about 4% globally this year, at the higher end of its previous forecast.
